title: Batch Process Deviation Alerting
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="batch:phase" batch_id=*
| lookup recipe_limits recipe phase OUTPUT min_temp max_temp max_duration_sec
| where temp_c < min_temp OR temp_c > max_temp OR phase_duration_sec > max_duration_sec
| stats latest(batch_id) as batch, values(phase) as phases by reactor_id
| sort -_time
description: Recipe phase duration, temperature, or agitator speed outside limits risks scrap or unsafe reactions.
value: Recipe phase duration, temperature, or agitator speed outside limits risks scrap or unsafe reactions.
implementation: Integrate with quality hold workflow; electronic signatures for parameter overrides.
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance—triage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Batch executive (S88), historian.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="batch:phase"`, `sourcetype="historian:sample"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate with quality hold workflow; electronic signatures for parameter overrides.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="batch:phase" batch_id=*
| lookup recipe_limits recipe phase OUTPUT min_temp max_temp max_duration_sec
| where temp_c < min_temp OR temp_c > max_temp OR phase_duration_sec > max_duration_sec
| stats latest(batch_id) as batch, values(phase) as phases by reactor_id
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Batch Process Deviation Alerting** — Recipe phase duration, temperature, or agitator speed outside limits risks scrap or unsafe reactions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="batch:phase"`, `sourcetype="historian:sample"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Batch executive (S88), historian. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: batch:phase. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="batch:phase". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where temp_c < min_temp OR temp_c > max_temp OR phase_duration_sec > max_duration_sec` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reactor_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (deviations), Control chart (temp), Timeline (phases).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: batch process deviation alerting before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
